close_reason: Positive observation: spawn_blocking for keyring syscalls is correct
description:
secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:116 (get) and 158 (put) wrap all keyring crate calls in `tokio::task::spawn_blocking`. The keyring crate's keyutils backend invokes `add_key(2)`, `request_key(2)`, and `keyctl(2)` syscalls synchronously. While these are usually fast (microseconds), they CAN block under memory pressure or when interacting with kernel locks held by another process; calling them on a tokio worker thread would stall other tasks on the same thread.

This is the right call. Other backends in the workspace use the same pattern (secretx-desktop, secretx-pkcs11, secretx-wolfhsm — see comparison in note on this epic). The defensible position is: any synchronous-blocking native API wrapped in a tokio impl belongs in spawn_blocking. A reviewer suggesting 'this syscall is fast, just call it inline' is wrong — fast 99% of the time is not 'fast enough' for an async runtime, where worst-case starvation is what matters.

The comments at lib.rs:114-115 and 150 explicitly state the reasoning. Preserve those comments.
title: Decision: spawn_blocking around keyring syscalls (correct async hygiene)
